page: Vultr vc2-16c-64gb is a General Purpose cloud server belonging to the Cloud Compute family. It features 16 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.0 GHz, with 16 physical cores and a shared CPU allocation model. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled as a 1280 GB NVMe SSD, and the server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present on this instance.

Performance benchmarks place this server consistently in the average tier across most standard workloads. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance is measured at 1451.59, while multi-core performance reaches 21535.52. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 1518.77 GB/sec, dropping to 107.12 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, with large block write speeds at 53.71 GB/sec. Single-threaded gzip compression is a relative bottleneck, scoring in the weak tier at 23.39 GB/sec, though multi-threaded bzip3 decompression performs adequately at 66.52 GB/sec.

Given its shared CPU allocation, this server offers a cost-effective option for resource-dense workloads that do not require dedicated CPU threads. The combination of 64.0 GB of RAM and 1280 GB of local NVMe storage makes it suitable for general-purpose applications, static web serving with 12.01 GB/sec throughput, database operations scoring 4649.2 on PassMark database tests, and Redis caching deployments, where it handles 23.04 million SET operations per second.
description: Vultr vc2-16c-64gb is a general-purpose cloud server from the Cloud Compute family, configured with 16 vCPUs on a shared Intel Xeon x86_64 architecture running at 2.0 GHz. It features 64.0 GB of RAM (4.0 GB per core) and a bundled 1280 GB NVMe SSD for local storage, with no GPU hardware. Benchmark results indicate average performance across most metrics, including a PassMark CPU score of 21511.0 and a multi-core stress-ng score of 21535.52. Memory bandwidth is measured at 107.12 GB/sec for large uncached reads, while static web serving throughput reaches 12.01 GB/sec. Due to its shared CPU allocation, this server provides a cost-effective balance of memory and storage capacity. It is well-suited for workloads such as static web serving, database operations, and Redis caching.
og_description: Vultr vc2-16c-64gb is a general-purpose cloud server featuring 16 shared Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64.0 GB of RAM, and 1280 GB of NVMe SSD storage, delivering balanced performance for database and web serving workloads.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual server offering sixteen shared vCPUs, sixty-four gigabytes of memory, and generous local NVMe SSD storage.
